hypothesis: A diffusion generative model that is trained to jointly predict a differentiable surrogate noise-energy and its gradient and that applies a small number (≤5) of gradient-based relaxation steps per reverse-diffusion timestep (in place of HMC) will produce amorphous structures whose post-geometry-optimization potential energy distribution is statistically indistinguishable from the annealed training data (two-sample KS test p>0.05) while requiring fewer than half the denoiser evaluations of the HMC-denoising pipeline.
